title: Grants to Iowa Nonprofits for Historic Preservation Projects
description: Supports artifact preservation, historical research, building restoration for National Register properties, and interpretive programs including educational publications and public awareness events.

title: Community Investment Grants
description: Supports quality education, childcare, family support services, perinatal care, parenting education, home visiting, mental health services, employment advancement, and financial stability programs serving low-income populations in Travis and Williamson Counties.

title: School Garden Grant Program
funder: State of Nevada Department of Education
eligible_applicants: 501(c)(3) nonprofits with 2+ years school garden experience
description: Funds nonprofit organizations to implement school garden programs at Nevada Title I public schools K-12, including STEM curriculum integration, desert-environment-specific programming, community farmer's markets, and culinary demonstrations.

title: J.W. Couch Foundation Grant
description: Three charitable programs: Preservation (historical preservation priority for 2026, wildlife conservation, food/waste management, animal welfare), Education (early childhood, 21st-century learning, teacher support), and Wellness (mental health, digital wellbeing, outdoor engagement, mentorship). Programs must benefit local communities.

title: PLTW Launch Grants - Ameren Illinois
description: Funds implementation or expansion of PLTW Launch STEM modules in grades PreK-5. New program grantees receive $15K over two years, expansion grantees receive $5K. Covers teacher training, technology, equipment, supplies, and PLTW participation fees. Schools with majority high-need populations prioritized.

title: San Marcos Community Foundation Mini Grants
description: Quarterly mini grants for projects directly benefiting San Marcos residents. Single-year awards only. Organizations limited to two mini grant awards per calendar year. Cannot apply for both mini and regular grants in same quarter.

title: Emerging Needs & Nonprofit Capacity-Building Grants
description: Two tracks: Capacity Building (up to $10K for board training, strategic planning, fund development, staff training) and Emerging Needs (up to $5K for general operating, crisis response, sudden funding gaps). Must align with basic needs, economic programs, support for crime/discrimination victims, or non-clinical mental health services.

title: Mathews Community Foundation Grant
description: Supports innovative, collaborative, measurable, and sustainable programs directly benefiting Mathews County residents across community vibrancy, economic prosperity, educational success, and health & wellness.

title: Great Lakes Basin Small Grants Program
eligible_applicants: educational institutions K-12 and higher ed
description: Environmental protection projects applying ecosystem-based management to address watershed challenges. Projects must originate from approved community plans and address at least one Great Lakes Advancement Agreement goal. 18-month project term.

title: Youth Board Grant
funder: United Way of San Luis Obispo County
eligible_applicants: youth up to age 19 partnering with 501(c)(3) nonprofits, faith-based orgs, or schools
description: Youth-led projects with positive community impact. Applicants must work with an adult mentor and partner with an eligible organization. Projects reviewed by Youth Board peer members. All activities must occur in SLO County.

title: Vitality Grants: Arts Education
geographic_scope: Eight-county region in western New York (Genesee, Livingston, Monroe, Ontario, Orleans, Seneca, Wayne, Yates)
description: Supports PreK-12 arts programming across all disciplines delivered at schools or community organizations. Covers materials, support services (transportation, childcare, food assistance), and professional development. Must commit to participant surveys. Cannot fund field trips.

title: San Marcos Community Foundation Regular Grants
description: Quarterly grants for projects directly benefiting San Marcos residents. Single-year awards. Cannot fund religious or political purposes, support other charities through grants, or underwrite advertising/fundraising costs.

title: Emergency Solutions Grant (ESG)
funder: City of Orlando Housing and Community Development
description: Federal HUD HEARTH Act funding for homelessness prevention, rapid re-housing, and emergency shelter operations. Requires 100% matching funds (dollar-for-dollar). Reimbursement-based payments. Mandatory attendance at public hearing/technical assistance workshop required.

title: Rotary Club of Pensacola Community Grants
description: Small grants for local charitable organizations addressing Rotary's six areas of focus. Approximately 5 awards of $2,000 each. Manual application process with written submissions.

title: Arts for Oakland Kids Mini-Grants
description: Hands-on arts education at qualifying Oakland Unified School District schools. All art forms eligible. In-school programs strongly preferred. Elementary: minimum 16 weeks instruction with weekly classes. Overhead capped at 20%. Fiscal sponsors accepted without fee retention.

title: Kott Memorial Charitable Trust Grants
funder: Kott Memorial Charitable Trust (via Oak Park-River Forest Community Foundation)
geographic_scope: Northern Illinois (including Chicago Metro) and Southeastern Wisconsin
description: Supports elderly services including access to housing, aging in place, healthcare access, community enrichment, food security, and legal assistance. One-year grants. Cannot fund endowments, technology access, or financial insecurity/fraud prevention programs.

title: Greater Lawrence Summer Fund
geographic_scope: Lawrence, Methuen, Andover, North Andover, Massachusetts
description: Summer programs enhancing social, academic, and emotional abilities of Greater Lawrence youth. Promotes safety, health, skill development, reduces summer learning loss, celebrates racial and cultural identity, and builds youth-staff relationships. Average award $6,000. Residential camps must meet ACA guidelines.

title: Community Violence Intervention Services RFP
funder: City of Oakland Department of Violence Prevention
description: Three-year awards for violence intervention services. Gun Violence Strategy receives ~$7.8M/year (60%), Gender-Based Violence Strategy receives ~$5.2M/year (40%). Must serve individuals at highest risk of or involved in violence.

title: Transform Lake County (TLC) Grant
description: Two tracks: Programmatic ($2K-$35K) and Capacity Building (up to $10K for strategic planning, technology upgrades, board/staff training, program evaluation). Four focus areas: Basic Needs, Economic Mobility, Community Enrichment, Safety and Inclusive Justice.

title: Main Street Community Foundation Grants
geographic_scope: Bristol, Burlington, Plainville, Plymouth, Southington, Wolcott, Connecticut
description: Programs increasing access to opportunity, basic needs support, and community vitality. Pre-application discussion with staff required at least 1 week before deadline. Cannot fund operating deficits, endowments, property repairs, or religious activities. Cannot have received funding for 2+ consecutive years.

title: Russell County Area Community Foundation Grants
description: Multiple grant types with different cycles. Community Impact, Education, Public Health have spring/fall deadlines. Disaster Relief, Youth Sports, Extracurricular Activities accepted year-round. All projects must complete within one year.

title: Neighborhood Grant Program
description: Tiered funding based on neighborhood size: under 250 households ($1,500 max), 251-499 ($3,000 max), 500+ ($5,000 max). Block party grants $500 max. Supports youth programs, volunteer projects, and neighborhood events.

title: Operation Round-Up Grant
description: Quarterly grants supporting programs, projects, and activities that support community health and wellbeing. One award per four-quarter period per organization.

title: SEMAC Arts Programs Funding
description: Supports creation, performance, publication, and exhibition of arts in southeastern Minnesota. Budget proposals should prioritize paying artists and creative professionals. Biannual deadlines.

title: North Smithfield Ambulance and Rescue Association Fund
description: Small grants improving health and well-being of North Smithfield residents. Priority given to organizations without access to other funding sources.

title: Community Development Block Grant (CDBG)
funder: City of Utica Department of Urban & Economic Development
description: Federal CDBG funds for activities serving low/moderate-income Utica residents. Max 15% for public/human services. Must meet national objectives: low/moderate-income benefit or elimination of slums/blight. Eligible activities include senior centers, transportation, after-school, tutoring, housing rehab.

title: Middlesex United Way Community Impact Grants
description: Five Community Impact Areas: Youth Opportunity, Healthy Communities, Financial Security, Community Resiliency, and Equity. Applicants must attend four quarterly community gatherings. Funds expended July 2026 - June 2027.

title: Being for Others Health and Wellness Foundation Grants
description: Eight priority pillars addressing social determinants of health. One-year contracts. Supports new and existing work including expansion, capacity building, and pilot programs. Three application cycles per year.

title: South Cumberland Community Fund Grants
geographic_scope: Grundy, Marion, and Franklin counties, Tennessee
description: Three tiers: Spring (up to $10K), Summer (up to $50K with LOI), Fall (up to $5K), plus rolling capacity-building grants. Focus on health, education, and economic development. Must attend mandatory info session. Grant reports every six months.

title: Ellison Foundation Education Grants
eligible_applicants: K-12 public or charter school educators certified in Washington
description: Two categories: Academic Enrichment (individual teachers or teams) and Professional Learning (teacher teams only). 15-20 grants expected. Covers books, materials, supplies, technology, stipends, substitute fees. Must be used during 2026-2027 school year.

title: Summer Food Service Program Expansion Grant
funder: Nebraska Department of Education - Nutrition Services
description: Nonrecurring costs for expanding or initiating Summer Food Service Programs in high-need areas (50%+ free/reduced-price lunch or 50%+ child poverty). Covers equipment, facility modifications, staff training, outreach, transportation. Cannot fund food itself.

title: Philadelphia Forfeiture Fund Grants
description: Grants for organizations helping Philadelphia residents escape poverty and avoid crime. Must select one focus area per application. Average award $20K. Maximum 100 applications accepted. Priority given to smaller organizations.

title: Hoyt Foundation Community Grants
funder: Stewart W. and Willma C. Hoyt Foundation
eligible_applicants: 501(c)(3) nonprofits located in or serving Broome County
description: Supports health, arts, education, humanities, and human services. Pre-application contact mandatory (phone inquiry one month before, in-person meeting with CEO before full proposal). One proposal per org per calendar year. Two-year wait between awards.

title: Toledo Rotary Foundation Grants
geographic_scope: Toledo and Northwest Ohio (all Ohio eligible)
description: ~20 grants annually across six focus areas. Priority given to multi-organizational collaborations. Two-year wait between awards for recipients. Cannot fund general operating, retroactive expenses, endowments, or capital campaigns.

title: Fred C. and Katherine B. Andersen Foundation Grants
funder: Fred C. and Katherine B. Andersen Foundation
geographic_scope: Washington and eastern Ramsey County (MN); Polk, St. Croix, and Pierce counties (WI)
description: Priority areas: healthcare, housing, education, human services, civic projects, environmental education. Limited ability to fund newly established organizations. Three annual deadlines. Cannot fund endowments, individuals, political campaigns, sponsorships, or lobbying.

title: Travis County Youth Education Mini-Grants
description: Supports small nonprofits providing after-school tutoring, mentoring, and academic enrichment for underserved youth in Travis County. Priority given to organizations serving first-generation and Latino students. Single online application, no LOI required. Awards are one-year, non-renewable.

title: Manos Unidas Education Grants
description: National grants for nonprofits running academic support and mentoring programs for Latino youth, particularly first-generation college-bound students. Simple two-page narrative application. Organizations with budgets under $250K preferred. One-year awards for direct program costs.

title: Austin Area Rotary Club Community Service Grants
description: Small grants for Austin-area nonprofits addressing youth development and educational access. Preference for organizations with budgets under $200K. Short online application (2 pages max). Funds may be used for supplies, program materials, and direct service costs.

title: Rhode Island Food Security Small Grants
funder: Rhode Island Foundation — Fresh Start Fund
description: Micro-grants for community-based food access projects in Providence County. Supports community gardens, farmers markets, nutrition workshops, and food distribution in underserved areas. Simple one-page online application. No prior grant experience required. First-time applicants encouraged.

title: Blackstone Valley Community Garden Grants
geographic_scope: Northern Providence County, Rhode Island (including North Smithfield, Woonsocket, Cumberland, Lincoln)
description: Supports establishment and expansion of community gardens in northern Providence County food deserts. Covers seeds, tools, raised beds, irrigation, and nutrition education materials. Short application (2 pages). Volunteer-run organizations welcome. No multi-year commitment required.

title: Steuben County Youth Opportunity Grants
funder: Steuben County Community Foundation — Youth First Fund
description: Grants for youth-serving nonprofits in Steuben County. Supports mentoring, leadership development, life skills training, and structured after-school programming. Organizations with fewer than 3 years of operation are eligible and encouraged to apply. One-year awards.

title: Indiana Rural Youth Mentoring Initiative
description: Supports evidence-based and emerging mentoring programs in rural Indiana. Newer organizations (1-3 years old) explicitly welcomed. Covers mentor training, program supplies, background checks, and activity costs. Simple narrative application with budget template provided.

title: Cook County Senior Services Enhancement Grants
funder: Suburban Area Agency on Aging — Cook County
description: Supports expansion of home-delivered meals, congregate dining, social engagement, and transportation services for older adults in suburban Cook County. Priority for programs addressing social isolation among homebound seniors. One-year awards with option for renewal.

title: Oak Park Community Foundation Aging Well Grants
geographic_scope: Oak Park and River Forest, Illinois (Cook County)
description: Grants for organizations providing direct services to older adults in Oak Park and River Forest. Supports meal delivery, wellness programs, social activities, caregiver respite, and home modification assistance. LOI is one page; full application invited within 3 weeks.

title: Escambia County Adult Education Grants
description: Supports organizations providing adult education services including GED preparation, literacy tutoring, English language classes, and basic workforce readiness in Escambia County. Programs serving adults 18+ only. One-year project period.

title: Northwest Florida Literacy Coalition Grants
geographic_scope: Northwest Florida (Escambia, Santa Rosa, Okaloosa, Walton counties)
description: Grants for adult literacy and continuing education programs in Northwest Florida. Covers instructional materials, tutor training, technology for learners, and program coordination. Priority given to programs demonstrating measurable outcomes in reading level advancement or GED completion.

title: Lake County Economic Mobility Grants
description: Supports programs helping adults overcome barriers to employment, including formerly incarcerated individuals. Covers job training, certification programs, resume assistance, interview preparation, and employer partnership development. One-year awards.

title: Tennessee Conservation Small Grants
description: Supports grassroots conservation and environmental education projects across Tennessee. Covers habitat restoration, water monitoring, invasive species removal, nature-based education programs, and community science initiatives. Rural organizations prioritized.

title: Philadelphia Responsible Fatherhood Grants
funder: William Penn Foundation — Family Strengthening Initiative
description: Supports organizations operating dedicated fatherhood programs including parenting workshops, co-parenting mediation, father-child bonding activities, job readiness for fathers, and family court navigation. Must serve fathers as the primary population, not general family services.

title: East Bay STEM Equity Initiative
description: Supports organizations providing STEM and technology education to underrepresented youth in the East Bay, with priority for programs serving girls of color. Covers curriculum development, equipment, instructor costs, and field experiences. Programs must demonstrate culturally responsive pedagogy.

title: Connecticut Arts Education Youth Grants
funder: Connecticut Community Foundation — Arts for All Fund
geographic_scope: Middlesex and New Haven Counties, Connecticut
description: Supports youth arts education programs in Middlesex and New Haven Counties. All disciplines eligible including music, visual arts, theater, dance, creative writing, and digital media. Programs must serve youth ages 5-18. Priority for underserved communities.

title: San Diego County Veteran Wellness Grants
description: Supports innovative veteran mental health and wellness programs in San Diego County, including animal-assisted therapy, equine therapy, art therapy, peer support networks, and nature-based healing. Programs must be veteran-specific and evidence-informed.

title: Cass County Indigenous Heritage Grants
funder: Northwest Minnesota Foundation — Native Voices Fund
geographic_scope: Cass, Beltrami, Hubbard, and Itasca Counties, Minnesota
description: Supports indigenous-led programs preserving cultural traditions, language, and traditional ecological knowledge in north-central Minnesota. Covers elder-youth knowledge transfer, wild rice and maple sugar education, native plant restoration, and cultural arts. Tribal organizations and indigenous-led nonprofits prioritized.

title: Federal Adult Education and Family Literacy Grants
funder: U.S. Department of Education — Office of Career, Technical, and Adult Education
eligible_applicants: 501(c)(3) nonprofits with 5+ years adult education experience
description: Federal funding for adult education and literacy programs. Requires 25% non-federal match. Extensive reporting requirements including quarterly performance data, annual financial audits, and participant outcome tracking. Mandatory attendance at annual grantee conference in Washington, D.C.

title: Williamson County Youth Achievement Grants
description: Supports youth education and mentoring programs in Williamson County. Programs must serve students in Williamson County schools only. Requires 5+ years operating history and budget over $300K. LOI followed by full proposal for selected finalists.

title: Federal Youth Mentoring Initiative
funder: U.S. Department of Justice — Office of Juvenile Justice
description: Federal grants for evidence-based youth mentoring programs. Requires 50% cash or in-kind match. Mandatory participation in national evaluation study. Quarterly reporting with federal compliance requirements. Three-year commitment required.

title: Rhode Island Community Garden Federal Nutrition Grant
eligible_applicants: 501(c)(3) nonprofits with 5+ years food security experience
description: Federal USDA funding for community food projects. Requires dollar-for-dollar match. Multi-year commitment (3 years). Extensive narrative (25+ pages), federal budget forms, logic model, and evaluation plan required. Annual site visits by federal program officers.

title: Cook County Senior Nutrition Federal Grant
funder: Administration for Community Living — Older Americans Act
description: Federal Older Americans Act Title III-C funding for senior nutrition services. Requires 15% non-federal match. Mandatory compliance with federal nutrition standards, quarterly reporting, and annual audit. Must participate in state-level coordinating meetings.

title: Allen County Youth Development Grants
description: Youth-focused grants requiring 1:1 matching funds. Applicants must demonstrate 3+ years of continuous programming. Serves Allen County only (Fort Wayne area). Not available to organizations in other Indiana counties.

title: Dallas County Latino Education Fund
description: Supports education programs for Latino youth in Dallas County. Awards $10K-$30K for tutoring, college prep, STEM enrichment, and mentoring. Strong program but limited to Dallas County — not available for Travis County organizations.

title: Pensacola Youth STEM Academy Grants
description: Funds STEM enrichment programs delivered in partnership with Escambia County K-12 schools. Requires formal school partnership agreement. Programs must serve students during school hours or in school-based after-school settings. Youth and K-12 focused only.

title: Greater Milwaukee Senior Meal Program Grants
funder: Greater Milwaukee Foundation — Elder Care Fund
description: Supports home-delivered meal programs and senior nutrition services in Milwaukee County. Organizations must demonstrate capacity to serve 100+ seniors monthly. Cannot fund programs outside Milwaukee County.

title: Appalachian Environmental Education Grants
description: Supports environmental education and conservation programs in Appalachian counties. Tennessee counties in the Cumberland Plateau region eligible. Covers outdoor classrooms, trail development, conservation curriculum, and eco-tourism development. Match not required but strengthens application.

title: National Fatherhood Initiative Community Grants
description: National grants for organizations operating dedicated fatherhood programs. Supports parenting workshops for fathers, father-child activity programs, job readiness tailored to non-custodial fathers, and family reunification services. Must demonstrate father-specific programming, not general family services.

title: Oregon STEM Girls of Color Initiative
description: Supports STEM education programs for girls of color in Oregon. Similar to Bay Area programs but restricted to Oregon-based organizations. Strong equity focus but not available to California nonprofits.

title: Southern Indiana Reentry Services Grants
geographic_scope: Clark, Floyd, Harrison, and Washington Counties, Indiana
description: Supports reentry and workforce development programs for formerly incarcerated adults in southern Indiana. Covers job training, mentoring, housing assistance, and barrier removal. Not available to organizations in Lake County or northern Indiana.

title: Florida K-12 Education Innovation Grants
description: Grants for innovative K-12 education programs in Florida schools. Requires formal partnership with a Florida school district. Programs must serve students in grades K-12 during or adjacent to school hours. Youth and K-12 focused; adult education programs not eligible.

title: Hennepin County Indigenous Environmental Stewardship
description: Supports indigenous-led environmental and cultural programs in Hennepin County (Minneapolis area). Focuses on urban indigenous communities. Not available to organizations in Cass County or rural Minnesota.

title: Arizona Animal-Assisted Therapy Grants
description: Supports animal-assisted therapy programs in Arizona serving veterans, children, and seniors. Covers therapy animal training, facility costs, and program staff. Arizona-only; not available to California organizations.

title: Greater Hartford Youth Arts Grants
description: Supports youth arts education in Greater Hartford. Requires 5+ years of arts programming experience. Serves Hartford County communities only — not available to Middlesex County organizations.

title: National Rural Conservation Corps Grants
description: National grants for conservation and environmental education. Requires 1:1 non-federal match. Multi-year projects (2-3 years) preferred. Supports habitat restoration, species recovery, watershed protection, and conservation education. Strong application but matching funds required.

title: Duval County Workforce Reentry Grants
description: Supports workforce reentry programs for formerly incarcerated adults in Jacksonville/Duval County. Covers vocational training, job placement, mentoring, and support services. Limited to Duval County; not available to Escambia County organizations.

title: Mountain Communities Health and Wellness Grants
geographic_scope: Grundy, Sequatchie, Marion, and Bledsoe Counties, Tennessee
description: Small grants supporting community health, wellness, and basic needs programs in the Cumberland Plateau region. Quick turnaround application. Supports a range of programs from health screenings to recreation to food pantries.

title: New England Community Garden Network Grants
geographic_scope: New England states (CT, MA, ME, NH, RI, VT)
description: Micro-grants for grassroots environmental and food justice projects across New England. Supports community gardens, composting programs, seed libraries, and food access initiatives. Very simple application (1 page). No prior grant experience needed. Volunteer-led groups eligible.

title: Colorado Springs Veteran Equine Therapy Grants
description: Supports equine and animal-assisted therapy programs for military veterans in El Paso County. Strong veteran mental health focus but restricted to Colorado Springs area only.

title: Mississippi Delta Adult Literacy Initiative
description: Supports adult literacy and GED preparation programs in the Mississippi Delta. Covers instructional materials, tutor stipends, and technology. Priority for programs in communities with low adult literacy rates.

title: Kern County Agricultural Worker Support Grants
description: Supports programs serving agricultural workers and their families in Kern County. Covers health services, ESL classes, children's programs, and legal assistance. Must directly serve farmworker communities.

title: Omaha Community Micro-Grants
description: Quick-turnaround micro-grants for community projects in Douglas County. Simple one-page application. Broad focus areas. Ideal for small organizations testing new program ideas.

title: Alaska Native Youth Cultural Programs
description: Supports Alaska Native cultural preservation and youth engagement programs. Covers language instruction, traditional arts workshops, subsistence skills training, and elder-youth mentoring. Alaska-based organizations only.

title: Detroit Neighborhood Small Grants
description: Small grants for neighborhood improvement projects in Wayne County. Supports block clubs, community gardens, safety initiatives, and youth engagement. Simple application process designed for grassroots organizations.

title: South Carolina Rural Health Grants
funder: BlueCross BlueShield of South Carolina Foundation
description: Supports rural health initiatives in South Carolina including chronic disease management, mental health access, telehealth expansion, and health education. Priority for organizations serving counties with health professional shortage designations.

title: Boston Area Immigrant Services Grants
description: Supports immigrant integration services in Greater Boston including English language learning, legal assistance, workforce navigation, and community building. Must serve primarily immigrant and refugee populations.

title: Georgia Youth Sports and Recreation Grants
description: Supports youth sports and recreation programs in metro Atlanta. Covers equipment, facility access, coaching, and league fees. Priority for underserved neighborhoods. Programs must serve youth ages 6-18.

title: Montana Tribal Language Revitalization Grants
description: Supports Native language revitalization and cultural preservation in Montana. Covers language instruction, curriculum development, oral history documentation, and intergenerational programming. Tribal organizations and indigenous-led groups prioritized.

title: North Carolina After-School Alliance Grants
description: Supports after-school and out-of-school-time programs across North Carolina. Covers program staffing, materials, transportation, and snacks. Priority for rural and under-resourced communities.

title: Hawaii Ocean Conservation Grants
description: Supports ocean and coastal conservation programs in Hawaii. Covers coral restoration, marine debris cleanup, ocean literacy education, and community-based coastal management. Match not required but strengthens application.

title: Wichita Small Business and Workforce Grants
description: Supports workforce development and small business support programs in Sedgwick County. Covers job training, financial literacy workshops, micro-enterprise assistance, and career coaching.

title: West Virginia Appalachian Arts Grants
description: Supports Appalachian arts and cultural preservation in West Virginia. Covers folk music, storytelling, traditional crafts, and community cultural events. Simple application suited for small organizations.

title: Louisville Reentry and Second Chance Grants
description: Supports programs helping formerly incarcerated individuals reintegrate in Jefferson County. Covers job training, housing assistance, mental health support, and case management. Must demonstrate evidence-based reentry practices.

title: New Mexico Solar Energy Education Grants
description: Supports solar energy education and green workforce development in New Mexico. Covers curriculum development, solar installation training, STEM workshops, and community energy literacy. Priority for tribal and rural communities.

title: Tampa Bay Homeless Services Grants
description: Supports homelessness prevention and housing stability programs in Tampa Bay. Covers case management, rapid rehousing assistance, employment support, and basic needs. Must serve Hillsborough or Pinellas County residents.

title: Vermont Farm-to-School Grants
description: Supports farm-to-school programming and school garden initiatives in Vermont. Covers garden supplies, cooking equipment, curriculum materials, and farmer partnerships. Simple application process.

title: Pittsburgh Youth Violence Prevention Grants
description: Supports youth violence prevention and intervention programs in Allegheny County. Covers mentoring, conflict resolution training, safe spaces, and community-based intervention. Priority for neighborhoods with highest violence rates.

title: Oklahoma Indigenous Language Preservation Grants
description: Supports indigenous language preservation and cultural programs in Oklahoma. Covers language classes, curriculum materials, elder stipends, and community cultural events. Tribal organizations prioritized.

title: Central Texas Federal Education Block Grant
funder: U.S. Department of Education — Title IV-A
eligible_applicants: 501(c)(3) nonprofits in partnership with school districts
description: Federal Title IV-A Student Support and Academic Enrichment grants. Requires school district partnership and 20% match. Mandatory federal reporting including quarterly expenditure reports and annual performance data. Must attend regional technical assistance workshop.

title: Bexar County Youth Mentoring Initiative
description: Supports youth mentoring and education programs in Bexar County (San Antonio area). Focus on Latino youth and college readiness. Strong program but limited to Bexar County — not available to Travis County organizations.

title: Pennsylvania Family Strengthening Grants
description: Supports family strengthening programs in Philadelphia. Broad family services focus including parent education, child welfare prevention, and family stability. General family programming — does not prioritize father-specific or fatherhood initiatives.

title: Idaho Rural Community Development Grants
description: Supports a wide range of community improvement projects in rural Idaho. Covers education, health, arts, and environmental programs. Simple application. Match not required but strengthens application.

title: Rhode Island Federal SNAP-Ed Nutrition Education Grant
eligible_applicants: 501(c)(3) nonprofits with 5+ years nutrition education experience
description: Federal SNAP-Ed funding for nutrition education programs. Requires 50% non-federal match and multi-year commitment (3 years). Extensive application (30+ pages). Monthly reporting to USDA. Mandatory annual grantee conference. Must follow SNAP-Ed Guidance framework.

title: Memphis Fatherhood and Family Reunification Grants
description: Supports fatherhood and family reunification programs in Shelby County. Strong father-focused programming but restricted to Memphis/Shelby County area — not available to Philadelphia organizations.

title: San Diego County Student Mental Health Initiative
description: Supports innovative mental health and wellness programs serving college and university students in San Diego County. Priority given to programs addressing anxiety, depression, and isolation among undergraduates. Collaborative proposals between institutions encouraged.

title: Student Success and Retention Grants
eligible_applicants: 501(c)(3) nonprofits partnered with institutions of higher education
description: Funds programs that improve student retention and graduation rates at San Diego County colleges and universities, with emphasis on first-generation and underrepresented students. Programs may include peer mentoring, academic coaching, emergency aid funds, and wraparound student services.

title: Campus Community Building Mini-Grants
description: Small grants for campus-based programs that foster belonging, cross-cultural connection, and community among students. Open to all San Diego County institutions of higher education. Simple application, no match required. Priority for programs designed by or with students.

title: Undergraduate Research and Leadership Fund
description: Supports programs that create undergraduate research opportunities and develop student leadership capacity at San Diego County universities. Preference for programs that serve students who would not otherwise have access to research experiences.

title: Basic Needs Security for College Students
description: Addresses food insecurity, housing instability, and emergency financial needs among college and university students in San Diego County. Funds may support campus food pantries, emergency housing assistance, textbook lending programs, and basic needs navigation services.

title: San Diego Higher Education Equity Collaborative
description: Multi-year grants for programs that close equity gaps in higher education access, persistence, and completion for students of color and low-income students in San Diego County. Cross-institutional collaborations strongly encouraged.

title: Career Readiness and Workforce Pathways
description: Supports career development, internship placement, and workforce readiness programs for college students and recent graduates in San Diego County. Priority for programs connecting students to San Diego's biotech, healthcare, and technology sectors.

title: Student Wellness and Recreation Grants
description: Funds wellness and recreation programs for college students in San Diego County, including outdoor recreation, fitness initiatives, mindfulness programs, and stress reduction. Priority for programs that reach students who don't typically engage with campus recreation.

title: Diversity in Higher Education Leadership Pipeline
description: Statewide initiative supporting programs that develop diverse student leaders into future higher education professionals. Funds mentorship, graduate school preparation, and professional development for underrepresented students interested in student affairs and higher education careers.

title: Transfer Student Success Initiative
description: Supports programs that ease the transition for community college transfer students at four-year institutions in San Diego County. Funds orientation programs, peer mentoring, academic advising, and social integration activities specifically designed for transfer students.

title: Campus Sexual Assault Prevention and Response
description: Funds evidence-based sexual assault prevention education and survivor support programs at California colleges and universities. Priority for programs that integrate bystander intervention training, culturally responsive support services, and campus climate assessment.

title: International Student Integration Program Grants
description: Supports programs that help international students integrate into campus life and the San Diego community. Funds cultural exchange events, buddy programs, off-campus community engagement, and programs addressing social isolation among international students.

title: Student Parent Support Initiative
description: Supports programs serving student parents at California colleges and universities, including campus childcare, parenting student resource centers, emergency financial assistance, and flexible academic support. Priority for programs at institutions with high enrollment of student parents.

title: San Diego County Youth STEM Pipeline
description: Funds K-12 STEM pipeline programs that prepare San Diego County youth for college and STEM careers. Priority for programs serving Title I schools and underrepresented students. University partnerships for mentoring and lab access encouraged but primary beneficiaries must be K-12 students.

title: Graduate Student Research in Public Health
eligible_applicants: accredited universities with public health programs
description: Supports graduate student research projects addressing public health challenges in San Diego County. Must be conducted through an accredited university public health or school of medicine program. Not available for undergraduate programs or student affairs initiatives.

title: Los Angeles County Student Success Grants
description: Supports student success and retention programs at colleges and universities in Los Angeles County. Priority for programs serving first-generation and low-income students. Applicant institutions must be physically located in Los Angeles County.

title: Federal Title III Strengthening Institutions Program
eligible_applicants: accredited institutions of higher education with high percentage of financially needy students
description: Strengthens institutional capacity at eligible institutions of higher education. Funds academic quality, institutional management, and fiscal stability improvements. Requires institutional matching funds and extensive federal reporting. Multi-year award with annual performance reporting.

title: Orange County Campus Wellness Initiative
eligible_applicants: colleges and universities in Orange County
description: Funds campus mental health and wellness programs at Orange County colleges and universities. Applicant institutions must be physically located in Orange County. Programs must primarily serve enrolled students.

title: Bay Area Higher Education Innovation Fund
eligible_applicants: accredited colleges and universities in the Bay Area
description: Funds innovative student services and educational technology programs at Bay Area colleges and universities. Applicant institutions must be located in San Francisco, San Mateo, Santa Clara, Alameda, or Contra Costa counties.

title: San Diego K-12 After-School Excellence Grants
description: Supports after-school academic enrichment and tutoring programs for K-12 students in San Diego County. Programs must serve students in grades K-12. Not available for higher education or college-level programs.

title: Undocumented Student Support Fund
description: Supports programs serving undocumented and DACA students at San Diego County colleges and universities, including legal navigation, financial aid advising, mental health support, and emergency assistance. Must be provided through an established campus resource center or equivalent.

title: Student Housing Stability Grants
description: Addresses housing insecurity and homelessness among college students in San Diego County. Funds emergency housing assistance, housing navigation services, rapid rehousing for students, and partnerships between institutions and local housing providers.

title: Veterans on Campus Support Program
funder: Bob Hope USO Foundation — San Diego
description: Supports programs serving student veterans at San Diego County colleges and universities, including transition support, peer mentoring, mental health services, and academic advising tailored to veteran students. San Diego has one of the largest veteran populations in the country.

title: San Diego Interfaith Campus Dialogue Initiative
funder: National Conference for Community and Justice — San Diego
description: Small grants for interfaith and intercultural dialogue programs on San Diego County college and university campuses. Funds facilitated dialogues, community meals, collaborative service projects, and programs that build understanding across religious and cultural differences.

title: Riverside County Student Mental Health Collaborative
eligible_applicants: accredited colleges and universities in Riverside County
description: Funds campus mental health programs at Riverside County colleges and universities. Applicant institutions must be physically located in Riverside County. Collaborative proposals between institutions encouraged.
